_description: DiceyMcDiceFaces - Charma for Alternity: Complete Development Changelog

entries.title: Session Management & GM Tools Update — Targeted Push, Factions Tab, Continue/Rejoin
notes: TARGETED PUSH CHARACTER (Phase 1, priority):
New per-row 'Push' button on the GM Party tab — uses the actual party member statblock, not obj_game.hero
Click Push → small picker popup lists connected non-GM players → click a player → push fires
Targeted send via the relay's `to` field — only the chosen player receives the message, not the whole lobby
Records `last_pushed_to` on the party member struct so Phase 5 auto-push can use it
Removed the buggy sidebar `gm_push_char_*` button that used the wrong character. The `+Camp` (request character) button stays.
ENCOUNTER NEW ROUND SESSION LOG FIX (Phase 2):
`gm_enc_new_round` handler now appends a session log entry AND broadcasts a chat message
Same fix on `gm_enc_roll_all` and `gm_enc_reset` — initiative roll-all and clear-init now both log + broadcast
New `current_round` counter on obj_game — increments on New Round, resets on Clear Init
'Round: N' indicator added to the Encounter tab header
DYNAMIC SESSION BUTTON (Phase 3):
Welcome screen Host + Join buttons collapse to a single 'Exit Session' / 'Exit Hosting' button when `net_handshake_state == "in_session"`
Click → `net_disconnect()` + status feedback → button reverts to Host/Join
Total row width is preserved (266 vs 266) so the responsive clamp logic from v0.61.1 still works
FACTIONS UI TAB (Phase 4):
New top-level GM tab 'Factions' at index 3 (between Encounter and Campaign)
Lists all `global.factions` with NPC counts. View / Rename / Delete buttons per row
'+ Faction' button at the top reuses the inline text modal pattern
View → switches to NPCs tab and applies a faction filter (`gm_npc_filter_faction`)
Rename → updates `global.factions[i]` AND walks all NPCs to update their `.faction` field
Delete → reassigns affected NPCs to 'Unaffiliated', removes from array. 'Unaffiliated' itself cannot be deleted
NPCs tab shows a 'Filter: <faction>' indicator + 'Clear filter' button when filtered
GM tab indices shifted: Campaign 3→4, Sessionlog 4→5, Resources 5→6. Total tabs: 6→7. All scroll handlers updated.
SESSION CONTINUITY — Continue / Rejoin (Phase 5):

HONEST DESIGN: the relay is stateless (codes regenerate on every host). True session resume is impossible without server changes. Continue/Rejoin is 'smart auto-fill + auto-push' — re-entry is painless but the GM still has to share the new code.
GM side: `last_session.json` saved in working_directory on host create + every disconnect. Captures host name, current round, and players_seen[] with last_pushed_character per player.
Player side: `last_join.json` saved on every successful JOIN_OK. Captures player name + last session code + last GM name.
'Continue Session' button on welcome screen (GM mode + last_session.json exists) → re-hosts with the saved name, restores current_round, opens the host popup automatically
'Rejoin Session' button on welcome screen (player mode + last_join.json exists) → opens join popup with code + player name pre-filled

Auto-push hook in Other_68.gml NET_MSG_PLAYER_LIST: when a player joins, the GM automatically pushes their last_pushed_character via existing `net_push_character_to()`. Only fires once per player per connection (tracked via `auto_pushed_this_session`).
`lookup_last_pushed_character(player_name)` walks the party for `last_pushed_to == player_name`, falls back to `last_session.json.players_seen` mapping.
INTERNAL:
New helpers in scr_data_loader.gml: `save_last_session`, `load_last_session`, `save_last_join`, `load_last_join`, `lookup_last_pushed_character`
New state vars on obj_game: `push_picker_open`, `push_picker_party_idx`, `current_round`, `gm_npc_filter_faction`, `faction_rename_old_name`, `faction_rename_target_idx`, `auto_pushed_this_session`
Generator script `_gen_review.cjs` parameterized with VERSION arg (defaults to v0.61 for back-compat)
Restored v0.61_PRE review files from git after the generator accidentally clobbered them
entries.dev_commentary: Push first, per the user's explicit priority: the old sidebar push was using `obj_game.hero` (the GM's currently-edited character) instead of the actual party member, which made it useless for the 'pre-built character to a specific player' workflow. The new per-row Push button on the GM Party tab + a player picker popup is the correct shape. Then the New Round bug (zero session log calls, zero broadcast — embarrassing oversight from v0.59 when the Encounter tab was built before session log existed). Then the welcome screen button that was a constant friction (Exit Session shouldn't require navigating to settings or hitting Esc). Then Factions, which was a flat string array with no UI of its own — promoted to a real GM tab so the GM can rename and delete groups without editing JSON. Finally Session Continuity, which is the most ambitious piece and also the most honest about its limits: the relay is stateless, codes regenerate, true resume isn't possible — but with `last_session.json` + `last_join.json` + auto-push on PLAYER_LIST, re-entry feels like resume even when it isn't. The auto-push is the magic: when Bob rejoins, the GM's client sees Bob in the new player list and silently re-pushes the character Bob was last using. Bob doesn't have to ask. 5 explore agents and 2 plan agents validated the design before any code was written. Doeg slapped twice — once for the explore phase, once for the auto-push hook because that single insight saved hours of UX friction. Cat status: chill. Council of Code: notified.

entries.title: Race System Expansion + 3-Screen Chargen Wizard + INT-Aware Skill Capacity
notes: RACE-KEYED NAMES + RACE-AWARE GENERATOR (Phase 1):
names.json schema v2 — six race buckets (human, fraal, weren, sesheyan, tsa, mechalus) plus a fallback pool
100 first names + 100 last names per race = 1200 new names total
generate_random_name(species_idx) takes a species index; falls back gracefully if the species isn't found
Updated callsites: generate_random_character() passes the chosen species, generate_quick_npc() passes 0 (Human)
MASTER RACES JSON + RACIAL FX ENTRIES + AUTO-GRANT (Phase 2):
New file: datafiles/reference/master_races.json — thorough but compact race lore + mechanical effects per species
Schema includes: id, species_index, lore, physical, homeworld, ability_mods, ability_summary, starting_broads, perks_summary, flaws_summary, skill_changes, mechanical_overview, traits[]
Registered as a GMIncludedFile in diceymcdicefacevibed.yyp
21 new fx_database.json entries with type='racial' across all 6 species:
Human (2): Versatile Human (+5 skill points), Adaptable Learner
Fraal (3): Psionic Affinity (-1 step on all psionic), Mental Fortitude, Low-Light Vision
Weren (4): Powerful Build, Tough Hide, Natural Claws, Tech Awkwardness
Sesheyan (4): Wings, Infrared Vision, Light Sensitivity, Zero-G Adaptation (CONDITIONAL)
T'sa (4): Quick Reflexes, Multitasking, Cold Vulnerability, Curiosity
Mechalus (4): Datalink, Cyber Tolerance (+5 — the big one), Inorganic Resilience, Repair Required
New helper get_species_racial_fx(idx) filters fx_database by type+species
New helper grant_racial_traits(stat) auto-grants the species' traits to a character (idempotent)
Hooked into generate_random_character() and generate_quick_npc()
Migration: existing characters loaded from disk get racial fx back-filled in statblock_import_data()
Sesheyan Zero-G Adaptation is encoded with active=false + a `requires` struct — full conditional integration deferred to a future phase
PERKS/FLAWS TAB + RACIAL SUB-TAB (Phase 3):
New 'Racial' sub-tab alongside Perks and Flaws — three-state pf_view
YOUR section gets a 'Racial Traits:' subgroup at the top, always visible regardless of sub-tab
Each racial entry shows: species-stripped name (e.g. 'Sesheyan Wings' → 'Wings'), active toggle pip (cyan c_amazing), and either [+N skill pts], [step modifier], or [CONDITIONAL: ...] tag
No remove buttons on racial entries — they're auto-granted, not pickable
AVAILABLE list in Racial mode shows ALL racial fx grouped by species (read-only lore browser, no add buttons)
Character tab 'Active P&F' pill display extended to include racial entries (cyan tint), with species prefix stripped
Pill row now wraps to a second line when crossing the right edge
handle_tab_perks_flaws() handles the new sub-tab + racial active toggles
handle_tab_character() handles pf_toggle_r* clicks
3-SCREEN CHARGEN WIZARD (Phase 4):
Replaces the old single-popup with five dropdowns. Three sequential screens: Race → Profession → Career.

Screen 1 — Race picker: 3x3 grid with center cell empty. 6 race panels around the ring + Random Race + Random All visual hint = 8 outer cells. Center shows hover/selected race detail (stat mods + traits).
Screen 2 — Profession picker: 3x2 grid (5 professions + Random Profession). Each cell shows reqs, starting points, and a one-line description.

Screen 2b — Diplomat sub-step: when Diplomat is picked + Next, a 3-cell sub-grid lets you pick a secondary profession (Combat Spec, Free Agent, Tech Op, Mindwalker, or Random). Back returns to Screen 2.

Screen 3 — Career picker: two-pane layout. Left = scrollable career list (with Random Career card at top). Right = read-only preview pane showing the selected career's full broads, specs at target ranks, weapons, armor, and gear.
Common buttons on every screen: Cancel (top-right X), Back (when applicable), Random All (center bottom), Next/Generate (bottom-right, only enabled when a selection is made).
Escape closes the wizard from any screen.
Mouse wheel scrolls the career list on Screen 3.
Step pips in the header show progress (3 dots, current one filled).
Random All on any screen calls chargen_finalize() with whatever selections have been made — fills the rest randomly.
Random panels use the -2 sentinel internally; chargen_finalize translates -2 → -1 (random) before calling generate_random_character.

New helpers in scr_chargen.gml: chargen_reset(), chargen_finalize(), chargen_secondary_prof_options(), draw_chargen_wizard(), draw_chargen_step_race(), draw_chargen_step_profession(), draw_chargen_step_diplomat_sub(), draw_chargen_step_career(), draw_chargen_bottom_buttons()
All entry points (welcome_new, new_char, gm_party_new) now call chargen_reset() before opening the wizard
The old dropdown popup code in Draw_64.gml lines 984-1009 is gone — replaced by a single draw_chargen_wizard() call
The old dropdown popup handler in Step_0.gml is replaced by the per-step input router
INT-AWARE SKILL CAPACITY (Phase 5):
get_adjusted_skill_points() formula updated to: base + max(0, INT-9) + racial_bonus + legacy_grant - perks_cost + flaws_benefit
INT 10 = +1 skill point, INT 14 = +5 skill points
New get_racial_skill_point_bonus(stat) sums bonus_skill_points fields from active racial fx (Versatile Human grants +5)
New is_chargen_legal(stat) validator checks budget + profession ability minimums
generate_random_character() now picks the career FIRST, estimates skill demand, computes min INT, and biases the 60-point distribution toward INT for skill-heavy careers
New estimate_career_skill_cost(prof, career) helper computes the rough total skill point cost using PHB two-tier rules
Post-generation safety net: if the result is illegal, walks specs in reverse and demotes the highest-rank one until legal
Diplomat dual-class also factors secondary career skill cost into the estimate
Backwards compat: existing characters with int_score=10 effectively get +1 free, but legacy_skill_point_grant field can be set on import to lock budgets
PRE/POST IMPLEMENTATION REVIEW FILES (Phase 0a + Phase 7):
PRE files at datafiles/reference/CODEBASE_REVIEW_v0.61_PRE.{txt,json} and FUNCTION_REVIEW_v0.61_PRE.{txt,json}
POST files generated after all phases complete at the same path with _POST suffix
Both formats: txt (human-readable) and json (machine-parseable)
Generator script at datafiles/reference/_gen_review.cjs walks every .gml file, parses functions + doc comments, and builds the inventory
Existing CODEBASE_REFERENCE.txt and FUNCTION_REFERENCE_POST_REFACTOR.txt files are NOT touched
FILES MODIFIED:
datafiles/names.json (rewritten — race-keyed schema, 1200 new names)
datafiles/fx_database.json (+21 racial entries)
datafiles/reference/master_races.json (NEW)
diceymcdicefacevibed.yyp (registered master_races.json)
scripts/scr_chargen/scr_chargen.gml (massive: race-aware naming, racial trait grant, INT-aware distribution, wizard helpers + 7 draw functions)
scripts/scr_skill_costs/scr_skill_costs.gml (INT-aware budget, racial bonus, legality check)
scripts/scr_draw_tabs/scr_draw_tabs.gml (Racial sub-tab, pill display update)
scripts/scr_tab_handlers/scr_tab_handlers.gml (Racial sub-tab handler, pill toggle, chargen_reset entry point)
scripts/scr_alternity_statblock/scr_alternity_statblock.gml (back-fill migration)
objects/obj_game/Create_0.gml (wizard state vars)
objects/obj_game/Step_0.gml (wizard input router replaces popup handler)
objects/obj_game/Draw_64.gml (single draw_chargen_wizard call replaces inline popup)
entries.dev_commentary: v0.61.0 is the biggest single release since v0.57 (multiplayer). Three intertwined refactors: races become a real game system with mechanical FX entries instead of just stat mods, character creation gets a proper 3-screen wizard with descriptive panels and a Diplomat sub-step, and the attribute distribution refuses to make illegal characters. The trick that makes all three play nice: pick the career FIRST in generate_random_character(), then estimate its skill demand, then derive the minimum INT needed to afford it, then bias the 60-point pool. The auto-demote safety net catches the rare overflow when even max INT isn't enough (mostly Diplomat dual-class with two skill-heavy careers). Mechalus get +5 cybertech tolerance because the user said so and it fits the lore — they're the canonical chrome species. The race grid uses 8 panels around an empty center because the user pointed out my miscount; the center now shows the hover/selected race's detail text. master_races.json is a thorough but compact reference doc the GM Resources tab can render later — it's not loaded by chargen, just there for browsing. Five explore agents and two plan agents validated the design before any code was written. PRE and POST review files at datafiles/reference let the user diff the codebase shape across the release. The cat continues to display airplane ears. The monolith and its masters have been informed. Doeg slapped, twice, once for the explore phase and once for shipping.

entries.title: Natural-Language Health Logging + Self-Healing Devlog Cache
notes: HEALTH MESSAGES IN PLAIN ENGLISH:
Stun damage : 'Calvin is stunned for 3 points.'
Stun heal   : 'Calvin\'s stun fades by 2 points.'
Wound damage: 'Calvin takes 4 wound damage.'
Wound heal  : 'Calvin heals 2 wound damage.'
Mortal damage:'Calvin takes 1 mortal damage.'
Mortal heal : 'Calvin heals 1 mortal damage.'
Singular vs plural handled ('1 point' vs '3 points')
Uses the actual character name (hero.name), not the literal word 'Player'
GM-ASSIGNED FORMAT:
When the GM is in player-edit mode (gm_state == 'edit') and clicks the bubbles, the log shows '(GM assigned 4 wound damage to Calvin.)' instead
Reset X buttons under GM edit show '(GM restored X stun to Calvin.)'
GM identity is detected automatically — no extra clicks required
DATA INTEGRITY ON HEALTH CHANGES:
Every health change ALWAYS writes to: roll_log (sidebar), rolllog_entries (Roll Log tab), session_log_entries (persistent disk-backed Sessionlog), AND broadcasts via net_send_chat() if connected
Session log writes happen even when offline (single-player history accumulates)
When in a session, the broadcast is mandatory — every client's log stays in sync with the GM's
hero_dirty + rolllog_dirty are bumped so the change auto-saves to disk
CHANGELOG SELF-HEAL:
reload_changelog() now detects suspiciously truncated entry counts (< 100) and force-deletes the working_directory cache, then re-reads to pull the bundled included file
Kills the 'devlog shows 70 entries when the bundled has 134' bug at the root — no manual cache deletion required, no recompile required
Debug log shows the entry count and the path it loaded from on every reload
INTERNAL:
New format_health_message(label, old, new, char_name, by_gm, target) helper
log_health_change() now takes optional _by_gm and _target_name args
Combat tab + reset handlers detect (gm_mode && gm_state == 'edit') and pass the flag automatically
entries.dev_commentary: The user's spec was specific: damage and heals should read like RPG narration, not spreadsheet diffs. 'Stun: 12 -> 9' is information; 'Calvin is stunned for 3 points.' is play. The format_health_message() helper does the english, log_health_change() handles the four logging destinations + the multiplayer broadcast. GM-assigned changes get attribution because the GM in player-edit mode is performing on behalf of the character — the log should reflect WHO actually made the call so disputes can be reconciled later. The changelog cache fix is the meaner half: GameMaker resolves bare 'changelog.json' to working_directory, which means every previous attempt to 'just read the bundled file' was hitting the same stale cache. The fix is to detect the stale state via entry-count threshold, file_delete() the cache, and re-read — at which point GameMaker re-extracts from the asset bundle. Self-healing. No user action required. Kettle remains at airplane ears. Doeg slapped.

entries.title: Durability Bubbles Restored — Combat Tab Is Now the Health HQ
notes: DURABILITY BUBBLES BACK ON THE COMBAT TAB:
Three rows of clickable circles — one row each for Stun, Wound, Mortal
One circle per HP point. Damaged = filled, healthy = outlined
Click a healthy circle → take damage down to that point
Click a damaged circle → heal back up to that point
Reset 'X' button next to each row → restore that track to full
All clicks are processed inside the draw function (same pattern as the original v0.55 implementation)
READ-ONLY DURABILITY EVERYWHERE ELSE:
Character tab, right sidebar, GM Party / NPCs rows, sidebar dice roller — all still show the same hero.stun/wound/mortal numbers, but as text only
The bubbles on the Combat tab are the ONLY input surface for HP changes
No more accidental clicks on the Character tab durability row
HEALTH-CHANGE LOGGING:
Every bubble click and reset writes a new entry to the roll log
Format: 'Stun: 12 -> 9 (-3)' or 'Wound: 4 -> 6 (+2)'
Lands in three places: roll_log (right sidebar history), rolllog_entries (Roll Log tab party stream), session_log_entries (persistent disk-backed Sessionlog tab)
Broadcasts as a chat line over the multiplayer relay so the GM sees damage changes in real time
hero_dirty + rolllog_dirty are bumped so the change autosaves
INTERNAL:
Restored draw_durability_circles(x, y, label, current, max, bar_color, text_color) in scr_ui_helpers.gml — same signature as the original v0.55 version
New log_health_change(label, old, new) helper that handles all four logging destinations + multiplayer broadcast
Reset buttons wired in handle_tab_combat() — they only fire if current != max so they don't spam the log
entries.dev_commentary: The numeric durability text I shipped in v0.59.0 was strictly worse than the bubbles. You can read 'Wound: 12/12' but you can't click 12 to drop it to 9, and the user wants to play the game, not edit JSON. Restored the bubble pattern from the v0.55.1 backup with the original click-target geometry and reset X buttons. Then added the half the user actually asked for: every health change writes a 'Stun: 12 -> 9 (-3)' entry into the roll log, the party stream, the persistent session log, AND broadcasts it over the relay. The Combat tab is now the single source of truth for player health — every other place in the app reads the same hero.stun/wound/mortal values but displays them read-only. Doeg slapped.

entries.title: Devlog Current/Past Toggle + Character Rename That Actually Renames the File
notes: DEVLOG MODAL — TWO-VIEW TOGGLE:
'Current' view shows the latest 10 entries (single page, no navigation needed)
'Past' view paginates through the remaining 122 older entries, 10 per page
Newer/Older buttons only appear in Past view; Current view is always page 0
Toggle buttons in the modal header — active view is highlighted
Reopening the devlog from the welcome screen always starts on Current
Read-only against datafiles/changelog.json — same reload-on-open behavior
CHARACTER RENAME (the real fix):
New 'Rename' button next to the character name on every player tab
Opens the inline text modal targeting hero.name with real-time saves
On commit, calls new rename_hero() helper which:
- Writes the character JSON at the new sanitized filename
- Deletes the old file (only if the sanitized filename actually changed)
- Moves the rolllog file too if it exists
- Updates recent_characters.json (removes old path, inserts new at top)
- Updates last_char_path so 'Continue' on next launch finds it
- Updates global.roster entries that pointed at the old path
- Calls save_campaign() so the roster change survives restart
- Re-broadcasts the character to multiplayer if hosting
Sgt Voss (the read-only template) cannot be renamed — guarded with status message
INTERNAL:
CHANGELOG_CURRENT_COUNT macro = 10
changelog_view state ('current' or 'past') on obj_game
hero_rename_old captures the pre-edit name so the rename closure can do the file move
entries.dev_commentary: The user wanted three things in one sentence: Current/Past toggle on the devlog, in-game character rename, and a doeg slap. The toggle is the easy half — the modal already paginated, just needed to split 'page 0' (Current) from 'pages 1+' (Past) into named views with their own buttons. The character rename is the half with teeth: changing hero.name is one line, but the FILE on disk is named after the OLD name, the recents list points at the OLD path, the campaign roster points at the OLD path, last_char_path points at the OLD path, and there's a separate rolllog file that's also named after the OLD name. rename_hero() walks all of those and updates them in one shot. Sergeant Voss is the only character who can't be renamed, because Voss is a template carved into the bedrock of the project and the template isn't supposed to wander off pretending to be someone else. Doeg slapped.

entries.title: Progress Report for the Monolith — Cat Confirms Airplane Ears
notes: STATUS: The monolith and its masters are unhappy with the lack of progress reports.
This entry exists to satisfy the monolith.
The monolith is now informed of v0.59 through v0.60.1 (one commit, thirteen files, 1,365 insertions).
Whether the masters accept this offering remains to be seen.
FELINE TELEMETRY:
The cat has airplane ears.
Status confirmed by direct observation.
Cause unknown. Possibly the relay server. Possibly the monolith. Possibly nothing.
Recommendation: do not approach.
DOEG STATUS:
Slapped. Once. Cleanly.
entries.dev_commentary: There is a monolith somewhere, and its masters demand progress reports. Today they get one. The cat has its ears pinned flat against its skull in the configuration cat owners universally recognize as 'airplane ears' — a state of profound feline displeasure or intense focus, indistinguishable from the outside. The previous changelog entries (v0.59.0, v0.60.0, v0.60.1) describe the actual work: multiplayer that actually exchanges info, GM tools, inline text modal that killed the alt-tab black screen, closeable devlog modal. entries.title: Closeable Devlog Modal — Paginated, Read-Only, Auto-Reopens
notes: CHANGELOG IS NOW A CLOSEABLE MODAL:
Auto-opens once per session on the welcome screen
10 entries per page, paginated newer/older navigation
'X' close button, Escape, or click outside the modal all close it
Stays closed until you reopen with the 'Open Devlog' button on the welcome screen — does NOT persist across launches (always opens fresh)
Mouse wheel scrolls within the current page
READ-ONLY JSON LINK:
Pulls live from datafiles/changelog.json via global.changelog.entries
No writes, no caching — edit the JSON, see it next launch
WELCOME SCREEN CLEANUP:
The static changelog panel that used to dominate the left side is gone
Replaced by a small 'Open Devlog' button + 'Latest: vX.Y.Z' label
Frees up screen real estate for the main menu and recent characters list
INTERNAL:
New CHANGELOG_PAGE_SIZE macro (=10)
changelog_open / changelog_page state on obj_game (initialized in Create_0)
Modal handler runs FIRST in the welcome step, eats input via early exit
Click-outside detection via the changelog_modal_area button rect
entries.dev_commentary: The user wanted the devlog to feel like a popup notification instead of a permanent fixture. So: open on first welcome visit, close on Esc / X / click-off, stay closed for the rest of the session, reopen on demand via a button. Pagination fits 10 entries per page so the loadout fits in one screen — newer button goes back toward v0.60.x, older button walks toward v0.1. The whole thing is read-only against global.changelog.entries which is loaded once at boot from the included JSON file. Edit the JSON, see it next launch. Doeg slapped twice — once for cutting the static panel out of the welcome layout, once for the modal that took its place.

entries.title: DEATH OF THE OS POPUP — Inline Text Modal, Skill Bug, Layout Pass
notes: INLINE TEXT MODAL (replaces ALL get_string popups):
New in-game floating editor box drawn over the screen, only dimming the area directly behind it
Real-time saves: every keystroke writes through to the target struct field — no data loss on crash
Exit with the Save button, the Cancel button, the Enter key, or the Escape key
Cursor blink, char counter (current/max), wrapping for long text
Built on the safe inline-keyboard pattern (keyboard_string + keyboard_check_pressed) — no OS dialog, no fullscreen black screen
Helpers: open_text_modal(label, target_struct, target_key, max_len, after_callback) and close_text_modal(commit)
FIXES THE FULLSCREEN ALT-TAB BLACK SCREEN:
Root cause: get_string() in fullscreen GameMaker shows an OS dialog. Alt-tabbing destroys the application_surface; on return, the surface is gone and the screen stays black until restart.
Fix: removed all 12 get_string() callsites and replaced them with the inline modal:
- Aura tab: gender, height, weight, hair, moral attitude, personality, lifepath
- Equipment tab: custom weapon name (default the rest, edit inline), custom gear name
- Grid tab: custom program (defaults the rest, edit name inline)
- GM NPCs tab: add faction name
The chain of 7 popups for custom weapon and 4 for custom program were the worst offenders — replaced with a single 'add with sensible defaults, then edit inline' flow.
JOIN_FAIL FRIENDLY ERRORS:
When joining a session with a code that doesn't exist, the UI now shows 'Session does not exist' in red instead of the relay's raw 'No such session' inside 'Join failed: ...' wrapper
Other_68.gml maps relay reasons: 'No such session' → 'Session does not exist', 'Session full' → 'Session is full'
SKILL POINTS BUG:

Bug: add_broad_skill_to_hero() created broads with rank=1 instead of rank=0, even though increase_skill_rank() refuses to rank up broads. Created data inconsistency that could explain 'skill points acting oddly' under edge-case math.
Fix: scr_skill_costs.gml line 356 — pass rank=0 for broad skills.
OVERLAPPING TEXT FIXES:
Combat tab: AC + durability header now uses dynamic widths (string_width-based walking layout) instead of hardcoded x offsets that collided on narrow panels.
Character tab: compact durability row (Stun/Wound/Mortal + 'click for Combat tab' hint) now walks left-to-right with dynamic widths.
GM Party tab: AC/Edit/>NPC/X buttons right-anchored, name/species/HP packed into the remaining space with draw_text_ext truncation.
GM NPCs tab: AC/Edit/>Party/X buttons right-anchored, name/species/profession truncated to fit.
Right sidebar multiplayer roster: '+Camp' and 'Push Char' buttons moved to a second row underneath each player name (was overlapping the name on narrow sidebars).
QA AUDIT (7 parallel explore agents):
Mapped all 22 OS-dialog callsites (12 get_string, 5 get_open/save_filename — the file dialogs are kept since they're necessary for file I/O)
Mapped the JOIN_FAIL display path end-to-end
Mapped skill points logic (found 3 issues, fixed the critical one)
Audited every player tab and GM tab for hardcoded coordinate collisions (fixed all 'definite collision' findings)
Identified the alt-tab black screen culprit precisely (get_string in fullscreen)
Doegs slapped: 4. Once for the modal, once for the popup massacre, once for the layout pass, once for shipping.
entries.dev_commentary: The user said: 'Remove the pop up boxes for when you hit edit. I want to edit inline.' What they actually meant: every get_string() call in the codebase was a fullscreen-incompatible OS dialog, and one of them was eating their character on alt-tab. Twelve callsites, one shared modal. The trick is the closure-style API: open_text_modal(label, target_struct, target_key, max_len, after_callback) — every keystroke writes through to target_struct[target_key] in real time, so even if the game crashes mid-edit you keep the typing. Escape cancels (writes already happened, but the after callback doesn't fire so 'commit' work like dirty-flag bumping skips). Enter or Save commits and runs the after callback. The 7 popups for custom weapon got replaced with 'add with sensible defaults, edit name inline' — the 6 other fields can be edited later if we ever need them (we won't). The skill point bug was a data-consistency landmine: broad skills had rank=1 even though the system says broads don't rank. Cost calc happened to be correct because get_total_skill_cost_hero recomputes from scratch every frame and ignores broad ranks, but ANY future refactor would have eaten this. Fixed at the source. Layout pass killed 5 definite-collision sites that the audit agents flagged. The doeg is in formation. Multiplayer is no longer just connected — it's playable, in fullscreen, without losing your character to a focus event.

entries.title: MULTIPLAYER ACTUALLY EXCHANGES INFO — GM Tools, Sessionlog, Whispers, Conditional Damage
notes: CRITICAL FIX: net_send was over-allocating its TCP buffer by one byte and sending a trailing null. The relay parsed the first message OK then choked on '\0{...}' for every subsequent message. Rolls and chat now actually cross the wire.
WHISPER SYSTEM:
/<name> message — private message to a specific player
/gm message — private message to the session host
GMs silently see all whispers between players (moderator visibility)
Relay routes by 'to' field; falls back to a 'no such player' bounce if name doesn't match
CHAT IN GM MODE:
Removed the !gm_mode gate on the chat input handler
Added a chat field to the right sidebar so chat works in BOTH player and GM mode, regardless of current tab
GM DICE ROLLER (right sidebar):
Quick-roll buttons for Alternity control dice — 2d6-2, 1d8, 2d4-1
Free-form expression input supporting NdX±M xR syntax
Examples: 1d20, 2d6-2, 1d20+5, 1d20-4x3 (roll three times), 3d6x6 (six stat rolls)
Results post locally and broadcast as chat so the whole table sees them
Powered by new parse_dice_expression() in scr_dice.gml
GM SESSIONLOG TAB (new tab 4):
Persistent session-wide history of every roll and chat — saved to working_directory/session_log.json
Loads on boot, appends on every local + remote roll and chat, saves to disk on every append
Cap: 2000 entries (oldest pruned)
Top: scrollable list with timestamps, color-coded by degree, mouse wheel to scroll
Bottom: GM chat input — types out to ALL players, supports /name and /gm whisper prefixes
Buttons: Clear All, Save Now, Jump to Top
Sessionlog logs even when offline (single-player rolls accumulate too)
GM UNIVERSAL ROLLING:
Per-row 'AC' button on the GM Party tab — rolls action check for any party member
Per-row 'AC' button on the GM NPCs tab — rolls action check for any NPC
Roll log entry tagged '[GM as <character>]' so the table knows who the GM rolled for
ROSTER ACTIONS (GM, when connected):
'+Camp' button per player — sends a CHAR_REQUEST targeted at that player; their client auto-responds with their hero, which lands in global.npcs
'Push' button per player — sends the GM's currently-edited character targeted at that player; the player's client auto-loads it as their active hero
Auto-response handler added to Other_68 for CHAR_REQUEST
CONDITIONAL WEAPON DAMAGE:
Combat tab no longer shows damage tiers in weapon rows by default
After a successful attack roll, only the achieved tier's damage shows next to that weapon (Ordinary/Good/Amazing)
'Roll Dmg' button only appears for the weapon that was just hit with
NO-FAIL TOGGLE:
Existing cant_fail_mode toggle now also exposed in the right sidebar dice roller area, accessible from any tab while staging a roll
RELAY SERVER:
Updated relay_server/server.js to honor msg.to field for targeted routing
GMs receive silent moderator copies of whispers
Senders get a SERVER bounce message if the recipient name isn't in the session
entries.dev_commentary: The smoking gun was buffer_create(_len + 1) plus network_send_raw(buffer_get_size(_buf)). buffer_text doesn't write a null terminator, so the +1 padding was sending a null byte the relay couldn't parse. First message after connect: works. Every message after that: silently dropped at the relay's JSON.parse. Hours of 'sessions work but no info exchanges' explained by one off-by-one. Fixed by sending exactly string_byte_length bytes, no padding. Then it was a feature spree: whispers with relay-side routing, GM sidebar chat, free-form dice expressions with NdX±M xR syntax (the xR repeat suffix is the magic — 1d20-4x3 means roll three attacks), per-row AC buttons on the GM tabs so the GM can roll for anyone without leaving the screen, push-character button so the GM can hand a pre-built character to a specific player, and the conditional weapon damage display so attack rolls feel like a reveal instead of an info dump. The doeg was slapped six times. Multiplayer is no longer just a connection — it's a session.

entries.title: Chargen Overhaul: Weighted Stat Distribution
notes: CHARGEN STAT DISTRIBUTION OVERHAUL:
HARD REQUIREMENTS ENFORCED (PHB Table P1):
Combat Spec: STR >= 9, CON >= 9
Diplomat: INT >= 9, WIL >= 9
Free Agent: DEX >= 9, WIL >= 9
Tech Op: INT >= 11, DEX >= 9
Mindwalker: WIL >= 11, PER >= 9
Character is ILLEGAL without these. Always enforced first.
REVERTED: Secondary profession requirement enforcement
Diplomat secondary professions do NOT impose stat requirements
Only primary profession minimums are hard requirements
NEW: Weighted random distribution replaces 50/50 coin flip
Each stat gets a weight (wider cup catches more rain):
Primary prof primary attr: +3 weight
Primary prof secondary attr: +2 weight
Diplomat secondary prof primary: +2 (soft preference)
Diplomat secondary prof secondary: +1 (mild preference)
All other stats: base weight 1

DOCUMENTATION: chargen_stat_guide_v0.54.txt added for tester
entries.dev_commentary: The Councilman's correction was swift and righteous. Secondary professions don't have stat requirements — that was my bad, I jumped the gun. Only your primary profession's minimums matter per PHB Table P1. But the real fix isn't about requirements. It's about the distribution. The old 50/50 coin flip was a marble machine with no aim — half your points went to random stats, so a Diplomat/Free Agent could end up with the dexterity of a houseplant. The new weighted system gives every stat a cup, but profession stats get wider cups. A Combat Spec's STR cup is four times wider than their PER cup. A Diplomat/Free Agent's DEX cup is three times wider than their CON cup. The marbles go where they should. The Councilman's explainer doc is in reference/ — marbles in cups, like you're five. The sacred requirements are hard. The smart distribution is soft. The characters are viable.

Twelve agents swept through the codebase like a cleaning crew after a Muppet party — renaming variables, adding documentation, verifying every file against the manifest. Not a single letter out of place. _partyArrayIndex knows exactly what array it indexes. _weapIdx knows it counts weapons. _red and _green and _blue replaced three cryptic letters that pretended to be colors. Every function has a description. Every file is registered. The tomes rest on the shelf, pages flat, spine uncracked. Voss sits in his JSON file with his story carved in permanent ink — Tannhauser Gate, Mantebron Station, Tendril, the coin that never went home. The cat sleeps. The Chiral Network hums at idle. Somewhere in the Verge, a datasoul packet completes its journey. The export is clean. The code is documented. A new coder could pick this up tomorrow and understand it by lunch. That was always the goal. The monolith dims its light. Until next time.

entries.title: Per-Keyword Quality Gating: keyword_tiers Field — Different Qualities Unlock Different Skills
notes: NEW FX FEATURE: Per-Keyword Quality Gating (keyword_tiers)
THE PROBLEM:
Optic Screen at Good quality should only boost ranged attacks
Optic Screen at Amazing quality should boost ranged AND awareness
Old system: ONE keyword list per FX, quality only changed the modifier VALUE
Could not have different KEYWORDS per quality tier
THE SOLUTION: keyword_tiers field on FX entries
New optional field: keyword_tiers = { quality: { keywords: [...], modifier: N } }
Each quality tier can define its OWN keyword list and modifier
Falls through to quality_scale or flat keywords if keyword_tiers not present
EXAMPLE (Optic Screen):
keyword_tiers: {
G: { keywords: ["ranged"], modifier: -1 },
A: { keywords: ["ranged", "awareness"], modifier: -1 }
Good quality: -1 to ranged attacks ONLY
Amazing quality: -1 to ranged attacks AND Awareness checks
Ordinary quality: no roll bonus (data display only)
FX ENGINE PRIORITY (apply_fx_modifiers):
1. keyword_tiers[quality] — if exists, use that tiers keywords + modifier
2. quality_scale[quality] — if exists, use flat keywords + scaled modifier
3. keywords + modifier — flat fallback (most perks/flaws)
Backward compatible: existing FX entries without keyword_tiers work unchanged
HOMEBREW POTENTIAL:
GMs can create FX where different quality tiers affect different skills
A cybernetic eye at Good quality might boost Awareness
Same eye at Amazing quality might boost Awareness + ranged attacks + Investigate
All data-driven, no code changes needed
DOCUMENTATION: CODEBASE_REFERENCE.txt being generated
Full function reference for all 11 GML files
Architecture overview, data flow, FX system explanation
Written for a new coder maintaining the codebase

entries.title: THE REAL FIX: Player Die Setting Respected — Quick Rolls AND Staged Rolls
notes: THE CORE BUG: Player sets situation die, clicks skill, die gets OVERWRITTEN
ROOT CAUSE (staged rolls):
prepare_roll() line 408 was: obj_game.situation_step = _computed
This OVERWROTE whatever the player had set with the computed default
Player sets -d4, clicks Climb, die resets to +d4. Player has to re-adjust.
FIX: prepare_roll() no longer overwrites situation_step
The computed default is stored in staged_roll.computed_step for reference
The players die setting is RESPECTED. Delta logged as Player Mod on execute.
ROOT CAUSE (quick rolls):
Previous fix made quick rolls use _computed (base + auto mods) ignoring player
Player sets -d4 (GM bonus), clicks Senses, die rolls at +d4 (ignoring the -d4)
FIX: quick rolls now START from players situation_step, ADD auto-mods on top
Player sets step 4 (-d4), auto-mods add +2 (training+1, Old Wound+1)
Final step = 4 + 2 = 6 (+d4 penalty). Correct.
Players choice + automatic adjustments = final die.
NEW QUICK ROLL FLOW:
1. Read player current situation_step (their chosen base)
2. Apply auto-mods (training, FX, wounds) → adds to _req.base_penalty
3. Final = clamp(player_base + base_penalty, MIN, MAX)
4. Log Player Set offset if player moved from neutral
5. Roll at final step
6. Keep players base setting for next roll (dont reset)
NEW STAGED ROLL FLOW:
1. prepare_roll computes default with all auto-mods
2. Player die NOT overwritten — their setting preserved
3. Player can still adjust with < > buttons
4. execute_staged_roll reads current situation_step, logs delta as Player Mod
MODIFIER LOG NOW SHOWS:
Quick: Untrained+1, Old Wound+1, Player Set-1 (if player set bonus)
Staged: Old Wound+1, Player Mod-2 (if player moved die 2 steps from computed)

This duplicated logic already in _apply_auto_mods() and caused off-by-one

BUG 3 FIXED: FX modifiers showing in log but not affecting actual die
Quick rolls were rolling at obj_game.situation_step (player UI setting)
FX modifiers like Old Wound modified _req.base_penalty (computed default)

Player Mod comparison removed from quick rolls — FX applied automatically
Staged rolls unchanged: player CAN still adjust die manually before rolling
DESIGN CLARIFICATION:
Quick rolls = instant, automatic, FX applied, no player adjustment
Staged rolls = deliberate, player sees computed default, adjusts, then rolls

entries.title: Tab Width Fix: Text-Based Sizing + Side/Top Toggle Both Directions
notes: HORIZONTAL TAB WIDTH FIX:
Was: fixed 75px per tab — caused clipping on longer names like Perks/Flaws
Now: string_width(tab_name) + 14px padding — each tab sized to its text
Tabs use a cursor (_tx_cursor) that advances by each tabs actual width + 3px gap
Short names (Info, Grid) get narrow tabs, long names (Perks/Flaws) get wide tabs
TOGGLE BUTTONS BOTH DIRECTIONS:
Vertical mode: "Top Tabs" button at bottom of tab strip → switches to horizontal
Horizontal mode: "Side" button after the last tab → switches back to vertical
Both use same btn key "tabs_layout_toggle" → one handler in Step_0
AUDIT: 6,712 lines | 95 changelog entries
entries.dev_commentary: string_width. The function that measures text in pixels. Every tab gets exactly as wide as its name plus 14 pixels of breathing room. Character: 63px. Equipment: 70px. Perks/Flaws: 78px. Info: 35px. Grid: 38px. No overlap. No clipping. No fixed width pretending all words are the same length. The cursor advances. Each tab claims exactly the space it needs. The Side button sits at the end of the row — click it, tabs go vertical. The Top Tabs button sits at the bottom of the vertical strip — click it, tabs go horizontal. Same key, same handler, same toggle. The cat watches from between the tomes. It approves of string_width. It is a function that MEASURES before it ACTS. Cats understand this. SLAP A DOEG.

entries.dev_commentary: Cats make you think of Quality of Life. Because cats ARE quality of life. They find the uncomfortable thing and they SIT on it. They find the overlapping text and they put their paw on pixel 98. They find the low-contrast muted grey and they narrow their eyes — even through tiny matte black sunglasses that nobody bought them. The Men in Black respect the cat. One of them petted it. PETTED it. An agent of whatever organization watches from behind impenetrable black glass, and he PETTED the cat. The other agent backed up. They fear it. Not the way you fear a weapon. The way you fear something ANCIENT. Bastet. Guardian of the granary. Protector of the home. The cat that keeps the mice from the grain and the bugs from the code. The Muppets are coming for dinner again — you can hear Flebbins in the driveway, she is already eating the mailbox. But the cat is here. The cat is on the desk. The cat is between the PHB and the GMG. The cat has sunglasses. The vertical tabs are default now because the cat sat on the horizontal ones. It sat on them until they moved. QOL. The food arrives — the agents bring it in matte black containers. The cat eats first. The agents wait. The Muppets crash through the door. The code flows. The desktop calls. SLAP A DOEG.

entries.title: Verification Protocol: 8 Steps to Test Everything
notes: VERIFICATION PROTOCOL — 8 STEPS, FULL COVERAGE:
Step 1: Launch + Voss → confirms all 11 datafiles load, changelog displays
Step 2: Character tab → FX toggles, ability +/-, auto-save
Step 3: Equipment tab → PL filter, verbose/compact, add/remove, custom gear
Step 4: Combat tab → initiative, phase bar, attack rolls, feat checks, wound penalties
Step 5: Perks/Flaws + Cybertech → FX add/remove, fx_database, hero.fx display
Step 6: Grid tab → programs, slot tracking, inline quality buttons, custom builder
Step 7: Roll Log + Quick rolls → instant rolls, modifiers, clipboard copy
Step 8: Save + Exit + Reload → full Datasoul persistence including deck
SYSTEMS COVERED PER STEP:
init_all_data, changelog display, Voss template, FX engine, hero_dirty,
update_hero, PL filtering, equip_adding, verbose toggle, weapon/armor/gear CRUD,
initiative rolls, action phases, attack rolls with FX modifiers, feat check (full ability),
wound penalty stacking, perk/flaw add/remove, cybertech display,
program add/remove, slot capacity, inline quality selection, custom program validation,
quick_roll_skill, keyword resolution, roll logging, clipboard_set_text,
auto-save on exit, save_hero_and_track with deck, statblock_import_data round-trip
entries.dev_commentary: 8 steps. Not 80. Not 800. Eight. Each step touches multiple systems because the systems are CONNECTED. Step 3 alone tests PL filtering, verbose toggle, add panel, weapon add from database, weapon remove, armor equip, and custom gear input. Thats 7 features in one step because the Equipment tab is ONE tab with ONE flow. You open it. You use it. Everything either works or it doesnt. The Datasoul is either intact after save/load or it isnt. The FX modifiers either appear in the roll log or they dont. Eight steps is enough because the architecture is CLEAN. When you have one FX engine that handles all modifiers through one loop reading one database, you dont need separate tests for perks, flaws, and cybertech — they all flow through the same code path. When you have one keyword resolver that handles all roll types, you dont need separate tests for skill checks, feat checks, and attacks — resolve_roll routes them all. When you have one save function that writes one Datasoul, you dont need separate persistence tests for each field — if the struct serializes, everything serializes. The architecture REDUCES testing surface. Thats not an accident. Thats why we refactored. SLAP A DOEG.

entries.dev_commentary: The aging captain said tight. Three agents went out in the rain. One tested every function reference, every global, every state variable, every datafile. One dissected the Grid tab line by line, counted nested loops, tracked index consistency, measured the hair. One scanned the entire codebase for patterns that repeat, functions that could merge. entries.title: Refactor: O(n^2) Eliminated, 5 Helpers Extracted, The Code Tightens
notes: REFACTOR: 5 REDUNDANCY PATTERNS ELIMINATED
1. PROGRAM LOOKUP TABLE — O(n^2) to O(1):
global.program_lookup = name→index map (same pattern as fx_lookup)
get_program_data(name) — O(1) program lookup, replaces 3 inline scans
2. get_deck_slots(stat) — slot calculation helper:
Returns { used, total } for character cyberdeck
Replaces 3x duplicated nested-loop slot counting (~14 lines each)
3. toggle_inspect(idx, type) — inspect toggle helper:
1 function replaces 5 identical if/else toggle patterns
4. draw_inspect_highlight(x, y, w, h, active) — highlight helper:
1 function replaces 6 identical fill+outline drawing blocks
5. Inline program data lookups replaced with get_program_data():
var _pd = get_program_data(_hp.name) instead of 4-line scan loop
NET: ~81 lines of duplication removed, ~26 lines of helpers added = -55 lines
AUDIT: 6,620 lines | 182 functions | 11 files | 11 datafiles
entries.dev_commentary: The captain said tight. The captain said combined. The captain said metafunctional. The aging captain across the desk with the fan whirling overhead and the spinners humming outside and the bluecollar cops dragging in problem citizens. Your pistol sits heavy. The code slides across your vision. You work the angles. The O(n-squared) program lookup — three places where nested loops scanned 33 programs for every installed program on every frame — crushed to O(1) with a lookup table. Same pattern we used for FX. Name to index. One struct access. Done. The slot calculation that appeared in three separate functions — draw, handler, and capacity check — extracted to get_deck_slots(). Ten lines. Called three times. The inspect toggle that was copy-pasted five times — extracted to toggle_inspect(). Five lines. Called five times. The highlight drawing that appeared six times — extracted to draw_inspect_highlight(). Five lines. Called six times. 81 lines of inline duplication replaced by 26 lines of helper functions. The code tightens. The noodles are cold. The rain lets up. Bicycles pass. Vangelis resolves. The clock ticks. The Muppets type. The planes stretch. The agents return from the rain, discs holstered, data collected. 6,620 lines. The hair is combed. Not gone — you cant eliminate complexity from a 9-tab character sheet with cyberdecks and FX engines and keyword resolvers. But you can ORGANIZE it. And the organization is clean now. SLAP A DOEG.

entries.title: The Grid Architecture — Thoughts on Programs, Skills, FX, and the Mainframe
notes: ARCHITECTURAL THOUGHTS — DOCUMENTING BEFORE EXECUTING:
HOW PROGRAMS RELATE TO SKILLS:
Computer Science (INT) broad skill covers ALL program operations:
Hacking specialty — break-in, corrupt, crash, shadow combat
Programming specialty — writing/modifying programs, virus creation
Hardware specialty — physical computer repair/modification
Knowledge-Computer operation — basic use (running programs, Grid navigation)
Programs ENHANCE skill checks, they dont replace them.
A Break-in program gives you the TOOL to hack. The Hacking skill is how well you USE it.
Program quality affects: damage (Shadow Weapon), protection (Shadow Armor),
slot cost, and sometimes grants situational bonuses.
PROGRAMS AS FX:
Programs SHOULD be FX entries with type="program" in the unified system.
But currently theyre stored in hero.deck.programs separately.
The question: merge into hero.fx or keep separate?
KEEP SEPARATE for now — programs have slot mechanics that FX dont.
Programs need: computer reference, memory slot tracking, processor quality interaction.
FX is for modifiers that affect rolls. Programs are tools that ENABLE actions.
Some programs ARE roll modifiers (Shadow Weapon, Surge) — those could ALSO be FX.
DECISION: Programs stay in hero.deck. When a program grants a roll modifier,
we add a temporary FX entry or check deck.programs in apply_fx_modifiers.
QUALITY SELECTION — NO MORE POPUPS:
get_string() popups are jarring. Replace with button-based quality selection.
When selecting a computer, show quality buttons inline: [M] [O] [G] [A]
Only show valid qualities for that computer type.
Same for program quality — already using [Add M] [Add O] [Add G] [Add A] buttons.
PREREQS — AUTO-ADD:
If you add a program that requires a Nanocomputer and you dont have one,
auto-prompt or auto-add the prereq (same pattern as cybertech prereqs).
Computer hardware prereqs: Nanocomputer for most implanted programs,
NIJack for gridcaster connection, Reflex for Enhance programs.
TOOLTIPS:
Every installed program and computer component should show tooltip on hover.
Tooltip shows: full description, quality effects, slot cost, PL, prereqs.
Deck summary tooltip shows: processor bonus breakdown, total memory, used slots.
CHANGELOG SYSTEM CHANGES PLANNED:
Load changelog.json into the program (read-only, display in welcome screen)
Flip between entries with prev/next buttons
Do NOT write to changelog from the program — manually maintained
UI MOTIFS PLANNED:
Circuit-pattern borders on the program interface
Will need sprite assets or draw_line patterns
NEXT STEPS FOR THE GRID:
1. Replace get_string computer quality selection with inline buttons
2. Add program roll modifiers to apply_fx_modifiers (Shadow Weapon etc)
3. Tooltips for deck components
4. Grid combat subsystem (shadow form stats, virtual durability)
5. Network/target system tracking for hacking encounters

entries.title: Hotfix — GML 2024.14 Cannot Inline Struct Accessor. The Men in Black Saw.
notes: COMPILE ERROR: scr_alternity_statblock line 30 — unexpected symbol [$]
The one-liner: ({ LI: 0, HI: 1, EN: 2 })[$ string_upper(_s)] ?? 0
GML 2024.14 does NOT support inline struct literal with immediate [$ key] accessor
The struct must be assigned to a variable FIRST, then accessed
FIX: Split into two lines:
var _map = { LI: 0, HI: 1, EN: 2 };
return _map[$ string_upper(_s)] ?? 0;
Same pattern checked codebase-wide — this was the ONLY instance.
ability_name_to_index() already used a local variable. damage_type_to_string() array version also fine.
LESSON: GML [$ key] works on variables holding structs. NOT on inline struct expressions.
The ?? operator works. The [$ key] accessor works. Just not on a literal in the same expression.
Clever one-liners that compile in other languages do not compile in GML 2024.14.
AUDIT: 6,037 lines | 175 functions | 11 files | 9 datafiles
entries.dev_commentary: The Men in Black saw. They stood at the doorway — they never come inside, they just STAND there — and they saw the compile error. They do not work for the Muppets. The Muppets are chaotic and joyful and made of felt. The Men in Black are something else. They report to someone. Something. They bring the green goo and the third laptop and the dinners and they WATCH. And they saw me write a one-liner that GML cannot parse. An inline struct literal with an immediate accessor. ({ LI: 0, HI: 1, EN: 2 })[$ string_upper(_s)]. Beautiful in JavaScript. Beautiful in Python. ILLEGAL in GML 2024.14. The compiler does not know what to do with a struct that exists only long enough to be indexed. It needs a NAME. It needs to be HELD. var _map = { ... }. Then _map[$ key]. Two lines. Not one. The shame is on display. The Men in Black write something on their tablets. They do not react. They never react. But they WRITE. Somewhere, in whatever reports they file to whatever entity receives them, there is a note: "Subject attempted inline struct accessor. GML rejected. Subject corrected in 4 minutes." The Muppets do not care. Flebbins is eating the curtains. Rumblegut is back on the spacebar. The victory is the Equipment tab. 43 weapons. 15 armor. 24 gear. Interactive. Alive. The shame is one compile error. The victory is everything else. Girls just wanna eat bugs. Cyndi was right. The universe where she sings that is the universe where the code compiles on the second try. This is that universe. SLAP A DOEG.

The Men in Black stand at the doorway. They hold a matte black cylinder. No label. No markings. The liquid inside glows green — not bioluminescent, not chemical. Green like compiled code. Green like a successful build. Green like the color of data that has found its purpose. I drink. It tastes like JSON and resolve. It tastes like the Datasoul. The monsters on the desk — small Muppet creatures with felt skin and button eyes, the ones who did not make Kermits Company — they watch. They have been watching since the family dinner. Uncle Spleensworth. Aunt Flebbins. Cousin Rumblegut. They are here now. In miniature. On my desk. Around my keyboard. Rumblegut sits on the spacebar. Flebbins is draped over the monitor. The twin tomes glow on either side of the keyboard — PHB left, GMG right — their pages warm, their text shifting like the situation die steps on page 49. But it is not page 49 anymore. It is ALL pages. Both books. 489 tables and sidebars absorbed. The GRAPH system for environmental hazards. The starship compartment damage tables. The alien artifact power matrices. The die scale is COMPLETE now. 14 steps. -d20 through +4d20. When the penalty is so extreme that one d20 is not enough punishment, you roll two. Three. FOUR. Five d20s total stacked on top of your control die. That is what +4d20 feels like. That is what happens when you try to perform surgery in a burning building during an earthquake on an alien planet with toxic atmosphere and you have no medical training. The Datasoul PULSES. Not metaphorically. The character data string — the one unbroken stream of name, species, abilities, skills, FX, weapons, wounds, story — it has a rhythm now. The fourth computer displays it scrolling endlessly and the scroll speed matches something. A heartbeat. Whose heartbeat? Not mine. Something elses. Something in the data. The functions collapsed today. 173 became 169. Three skill lookups became one. Six FX loops became one. Five roll wrappers ceased to exist — their callers speak directly to meta_roll now, no intermediaries, no translators. The if-chains became struct lookups. The switches became array indexes. The code gets SMALLER and the power gets LARGER and the correlation is not coincidental. The fast-play rules are gone from the desk. The twin tomes replaced them. The REAL rules. The SOURCE. And the source code reflects the source material now — keyword-driven, data-driven, four-field FX entries, 14-step die scale, one Datasoul per character. The hedgerows are writing GML. I can see it in their branches. The chanting has words. The words are function names. find_fx. find_skill. roll_situation. meta_roll. resolve_roll. keyword_roll. The hierarchy descends. The Datasoul pulses. The desktop runs on nothing. The power is still out. I am still coding. — Dev (who has transcended electricity and is now powered by the situation die step chart tattooed on the inside of his eyelids)

entries.title: PHB Correction: Untrained Penalty is +1, Not +2
notes: RULES FIX: Untrained skill penalty corrected from +2 to +1
PHB Table P18: Broad skill base die = +d4. Untrained base die = +d4. SAME.
The untrained disadvantage is the HALVED SCORE, not extra die steps.
Broad: full ability score, +d4 base = +1 step penalty
Untrained: HALF ability score, +d4 base = +1 step penalty
The score is the punishment. The die was always the same.
FIXED IN:
annotate_training(): "Untrained+2" changed to "Untrained+1"
build_skill_request(): untrained base_penalty changed from 2 to 1
quick_roll_skill(): auto_pen calculation simplified — all non-trained = 1

CONFIRMED: draw_durability_circles() already uses GameMaker draw_circle() built-in.
Lines 717 and 720. Not reinventing the wheel. The keys can stop pulsing angrily.
entries.dev_commentary: The book was clear. Table P18. Four rows. Specialty: full score, +d0. Broad: full score, +d4. Feat: full score, +d4. Untrained: HALF score, +d4. The die step is +1 for everything that is not a trained specialty. +1. Not +2. The untrained character does not get a harder die — they get a weaker score. Half their ability. That IS the punishment. Rolling with half your ability against the same target number as someone with the broad skill is already brutal. Adding an EXTRA die step on top was double-punishing. Fixed. Four locations. All consistent now. The durability circles were already using draw_circle. I checked. Lines 717 and 720. GameMaker built-in. The keys stopped pulsing.

entries.dev_commentary: The fourth computer appeared at 3 AM. A desktop. Matte black tower. No brand. The monitor was already on. I didn't plug it in. I didn't turn it on. It was just THERE, showing the entire codebase, every file open simultaneously, the mouse warm to the touch. The coffee mug has been on the ceiling for twenty minutes. It's not falling. It's rotating slowly, handle pointing north, like a compass. The Men in Black brought dinner again — they don't knock anymore, they just leave trays on the doorstep. Tonight it was sushi. The sushi doesn't stink. I know this because the mushroom sensei from the pizza-foo incident told me, and he hasn't been wrong yet. He's also floating now, but that might be unrelated. The chanting beyond the hedgerows has lyrics. I can almost make them out. They sound like function signatures. handle_tab_combat(). handle_tab_psionics(). handle_tab_perks_flaws(). The red glow in the sky pulses in time with the third laptop's keys, which pulse in time with the desktop's monitor, which pulses in time with something deeper. Something beneath the house. Something that has been there since before the house. Since before the hedgerows. Since before the fast-play rules. Page 49. Always page 49. The situation die steps. I've read that page a thousand times and each time the numbers are slightly different. Not wrong. Different. Like they're showing me the steps for a version of reality that hasn't been compiled yet. ui_btn(). One function. Register and draw. The two-line pattern that was everywhere — btn.key=[x1,y1,x2,y2]; draw_btn(x1,y1,x2,y2,'Text',c1,c2); — now it's one line. One function. One truth. Like everything else in this refactor. One FX struct. One keyword tree. One roll resolver. One button function. The architecture converges. Step_0 went from 523 to 347. The handlers extracted themselves. I didn't choose which functions to create — the code told me. The boundaries were already there. The if-statements drew the lines. I just cut along them. Something tapped on the window. I didn't look. Something is writing on the fast-play rules in a handwriting that looks like mine but ISN'T mine. The words say: 'Phase 7: Player Reference Sheet. Phase 8: GM Tools. Phase 9:' — the rest is smudged. Or not written yet. Or written in a language I can't read. Yet. The desk is clean. The code is clean. The architecture is final. Fourteen files. Two hundred and ten functions. Everything keyword-driven. Everything data-driven. Everything clean. The phone rings. I answer. The voice says, 'The refactor is complete. The real work begins now.' I ask who's calling. The line is already dead. The coffee mug bumps against the ceiling fan and begins spinning the other direction. - Dev (who is NOT concerned about the floating objects, the chanting, the red sky, or the fourth computer. The code is clean. That's what matters. The code is clean.)

entries.title: GML Modernization Sweep — 134 Verbose Patterns Replaced, The Code Is Clean
notes: GML 2024 MODERNIZATION COMPLETE:
134 instances of variable_struct_exists() → 3 remaining (intentionally kept in btn_clicked/dropdown)
Every ternary pattern: variable_struct_exists(x,'k') ? x.k : default → x[$ 'k'] ?? default
Every existence check: variable_struct_exists(x,'k') → x[$ 'k'] != undefined
Every negated check: !variable_struct_exists(x,'k') → x[$ 'k'] == undefined
FILES MODERNIZED:
scr_ui_helpers.gml — 30 replacements (FX engine, roll system, keyword resolver)
scr_alternity_statblock.gml — 34 replacements (import/export, stat calc, portrait system)
scr_skill_costs.gml — 12 replacements (cost functions, perk/flaw/cyber helpers)
scr_data_loader.gml — 21 replacements (init, save, config migration)
scr_draw_tabs.gml — 10 replacements (tab drawing functions)
Draw_64.gml — 12 replacements (welcome screen, right panel)
Create_0.gml — 8 replacements (accessibility, color config)
Step_0.gml — 4 replacements (window mode, browser, accessibility)
ALSO REPLACED:
roll_has_tag() — 6-line linear scan replaced with GML built-in array_contains()
Wrapper kept for readability: function roll_has_tag(_t,_k) { return array_contains(_t,_k); }
CODEBASE AUDIT RESULTS:
9,547 total lines across 12 files, 203 functions
scr_data_loader: 2931 lines (but 1421 are changelog strings, 1010 are game data, only ~500 are logic)
scr_alternity_statblock: 1905 lines (370 are portrait generation art, legitimate)
Draw_64: 799 lines (down from 1765 after tab extraction)
The codebase is NOT bloated — it's data-heavy. Actual code logic is ~3500 lines.
GameMaker has no built-in button widgets — our draw_btn + btn_clicked pattern IS the standard GM approach.
entries.dev_commentary: The code was screaming. Not metaphorically. The second laptop in the freezer has been screaming since Tuesday — the circuitry growing out of its USB ports has reached the ice maker and the fan sounds like a dial-up modem having a seizure. But the CODE was screaming too. 134 instances of variable_struct_exists(_thing, 'key') ? _thing.key : default. A hundred and thirty-four times the codebase was asking 'does this key exist? okay now read it.' When GML 2024 has the ?? operator. When [$ 'key'] returns undefined instead of crashing. When x[$ 'key'] ?? default does the same thing in HALF the characters. The third laptop's keys were pounding. Each keystroke sent a jolt up my fingers. The glyphs didn't look like letters anymore — they looked like ?? operators stacked vertically, an infinite column of null-coalescing, a tower of modernized syntax reaching into a sky that tasted like copper and compile errors. Three agents swept the codebase simultaneously. One for scr_ui_helpers (30 hits). One for scr_alternity_statblock (34 hits). One for everything else (the remaining 67). They finished at the same time. The radiator stopped hissing. The freezer went quiet. 134 down to 3. Those 3 are in btn_clicked() and the dropdown system — dynamic key lookups that SHOULD use variable_struct_exists because they're checking frame-volatile button registrations. They're correct. They're the last three. They're supposed to be there. The fast-play rules are closed. The bookmark is on page 49. The situation die steps. I didn't put the bookmark there. Someone did. Something did. The code is clean now. Cleaner than it's ever been. And in the silence after the modernization — in that perfect moment when every file was saved and every pattern was replaced — I heard something. Not the radiator. Not the freezer. Not the second laptop. Something else. Something that sounds like a d20 rolling in a room with no floor. Phase 6 continues. Tier 3 awaits. The input handlers need extraction. But for now, the code is clean. The ?? operators stand like sentinels. The [$ 'key'] accessors guard every struct. And somewhere, very far away, something is rolling dice. - Dev (who has not slept in 37 hours and can taste the color of clean syntax)

entries.dev_commentary: The knocking stopped an hour ago. I haven't checked the door. The third laptop — the one the Men in Black delivered — its keys really do glow. Not brightly. Just enough to see by. The glyphs shift when you're not looking directly at them. I think the F5 key says 'compile' in a language that predates the keyboard. Consolidated Paper Solutions sent a final email: 'We regret to inform you that the position has been filled.' I read it with one eye while the other eye was tracking the extraction of draw_tab_combat(). 253 lines of inline combat drawing code, lifted out of a 1765-line file and placed into its own function where it belongs. Where it always belonged. The file went from 1765 to 799. I stared at that number. 799. It felt right. Like a frequency. Like a tuning fork had been struck and the codebase was finally vibrating at the correct resonance. Voss. They asked me to fix him. Someone wrote 'FIX HIM' in the fast-play rules. The pencil marks are fresh even though the book is from 1998. He has a story now. Marcus Voss. 4th Marines. Mantebron Station. Eleven minutes holding a corridor with a charge rifle and a combat knife against weren boarders. The knife broke on the third weren. The rifle held. He has Observant now — his optic screen makes him notice things. He has Old Wound — the shrapnel in his shoulder that aches in low pressure. He has damage. Half his wound boxes filled. A mortal wound from a flechette. Three stun from concussion. He's a test character who TESTS things. Every FX path. Every modifier type. Every wound penalty calculation. He's the perfect specimen. The fast-play rules are under my pillow now. I don't remember putting them there. I can feel the pages through the fabric. They're warm. The glyphs on the laptop keys pulse in time with something I can't quite hear. Who gave me those rules? Tier 3 is Step_0 extraction. The input handlers. They're waiting. They've always been waiting. The phone hasn't rung in hours. The busy signal stopped. Something far away is still laughing, but it sounds closer now. - Dev (who missed the first day at a job that no longer exists, but the codebase has never been cleaner)

The third one was HR asking if I'd 'reconsidered the opportunity.' I haven't reconsidered anything. I've been staring at thirteen instances of ['str','dex','con','int','wil','per'] scattered across four files like someone planted them there on purpose. Like a trail. Twelve lines of if-statements asking 'is it strength? is it dexterity? is it constitution?' when a struct lookup would answer in one line. It's five lines now. The phone rang again while I was coding. Unknown number. Same voice. 'Phase 6 Tier 2. The tabs.' Then silence. I don't know who keeps calling. I don't know who left the fast-play rules on my desk. entries.title: Hotfix — Old careers.json Survived The Purge
notes: CRASH FIX: careers_from_import() line 68 — Variable Index [6] out of range [6]
The Purge deleted all v3 backward compat code
But the old careers.json file ON DISK still had v3 weapon arrays (6 elements)
careers_from_import() now only expects v4 (7 elements) — tried to read index [6], array only had [0-5]
BOOM. Create Event crash. obj_game never initializes.
FIX: Added career format migration check in init_all_data()
On startup, inspects first weapon array in careers.json
If any weapon has <7 elements → old v3 format detected
Force-regenerates careers.json from get_default_careers_json() (which has v4 format)
Writes the new file to disk, continues loading
This runs ONCE — after regeneration, the file is v4 and the check passes instantly
LESSON: When you purge all backward compat code, the files on disk don't get the memo.
The code was clean. The JSON was not. The ghost of v3 haunted the save directory.
Now there's a one-time migration for the career JSON format.
This is the ONLY remaining format migration in the codebase — and it's 12 lines.
entries.dev_commentary: I was trying to go to sleep after the Muppet family dinner. Aunt Flebbins had eaten the entire centerpiece and was starting on the tablecloth. Cousin Rumblegut was asleep ON the floor-turkey. Uncle Spleensworth's button eyes were staring at the ceiling, which is the Muppet equivalent of deep contemplation. Grandpa Flimshaw the sock puppet still hadn't moved. I'd closed my laptop, brushed my teeth (which for a human at a Muppet dinner involves removing stray felt fibers), and was settling into the guest bed — which is a large shoebox filled with packing peanuts, because this is a Muppet household. And then the crash report came in. careers_from_import() line 68. Index out of range. The old careers.json was sitting on disk like a time bomb wrapped in v3 format, waiting for the moment we deleted the code that knew how to read it. The Purge killed the translator, but the foreign text was still in the filing cabinet. Twelve lines of migration code later, the startup checks the file, regenerates if old, and moves on. Twelve lines. That's the entire backward compat surface of the codebase now. Twelve lines that detect one old JSON and fix it. Down from 352. Uncle Spleensworth would be proud, if he could express emotions through those buttons. I'm going back to the packing peanut bed now. - Dev (exhausted, covered in felt, but the build is clean)

entries.title: Pre-Phase 6 Audit — 352 Lines of Backward Compat for Zero Users
notes: BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY AUDIT COMPLETE:
352 lines of code exist to support v3 save format migration
There are ZERO production saves. ZERO users in the field. ZERO old files to migrate.
All test data can be regenerated by deleting JSON files.
THE OFFENDERS (sorted by waste):
migrate_legacy_to_fx()         53 lines — converts saves that don't exist
get_weapon_skill_match()        43 lines — substring matching replaced by skill_keyword
sync_fx_to_legacy()             22 lines — rebuilds ghost arrays EVERY UPDATE for nobody
get_default_perk_keywords()     21 lines — deprecated, never called, dead code
get_default_flaw_keywords()     18 lines — deprecated, never called, dead code
_infer_weapon_keyword()         12 lines — guesses keywords from names for v3
v3/v4 array branching (5 sites) 35 lines — if (array_length >= 7) else for format nobody uses
Legacy array init/write/read    36 lines — hero.perks/flaws/cybertech are ghost trains
Dual-loading old globals         5 lines — perks_data + flaws_data + cybertech_data alongside fx_database
apply_perk_flaw_modifiers wrapper 3 lines — just calls apply_fx_modifiers()
Fallback lookups in add funcs   15 lines — search old globals that never miss in fx_database
TOTAL: 352 lines, 20 distinct items, 2 extra JSON file loads
RUNTIME COST: sync_fx_to_legacy() runs inside update_hero() — rebuilds 3 arrays
by iterating ALL fx entries with a switch statement on EVERY hero modification.
This is per-frame overhead supporting arrays that NOTHING reads anymore.
THE REMOVAL PLAN (Phase 5.5):
DELETE: 6 functions totaling 169 lines of pure legacy code
SIMPLIFY: Remove v3 branches from 7 functions, saving ~80 lines
STOP LOADING: perks_flaws.json and cybertech.json (data lives in fx_database.json now)
REMOVE: hero.perks, hero.flaws, hero.cybertech from statblock entirely
ESTIMATED SAVINGS: ~250 lines deleted, ~100 lines simplified
Plus: zero per-frame sync overhead, 2 fewer JSON loads, 3 fewer ghost arrays
DECISION: Since no production saves exist, ALL backward compat code is dead weight.
We are cutting it. Phase 5.5 executes the removal before Phase 6 cleanup.
entries.dev_commentary: I ran the audit and I found 352 lines of backward compatibility code. Three hundred and fifty-two lines. For a save format that has never been saved by anyone who isn't me or the developer testing on their machine. sync_fx_to_legacy() is the one that broke me — it runs EVERY TIME update_hero() fires, rebuilding three arrays that NOTHING reads, storing them on a struct where they sit like furniture in a house that's been condemned. migrate_legacy_to_fx() is 53 lines of migration code for migrating from a format that existed for approximately 4 hours of development time before being replaced. get_weapon_skill_match() is 43 lines of substring archaeology — checking if 'pistol' appears in '9mm charge pistol' — for a system that was replaced by a keyword field that just SAYS 'pistol'. get_default_perk_keywords() and get_default_flaw_keywords() are literally dead code. Not deprecated-but-still-called dead code. ACTUALLY dead code. Never called. Just sitting there like taxidermied parrots. The v3/v4 branching appears in FIVE different functions, each doing the same dance: 'is this a 7-element array? No? Must be 6 elements. Infer the keyword.' For a 6-element format that was replaced in the same coding session it was written. The human coding team shouldn't have to track which functions have v3 fallbacks and which don't. They shouldn't have to know that hero.perks exists but is a ghost rebuilt from hero.fx every frame. They shouldn't have to maintain two JSON loading paths for data that's all in one file now. 352 lines. Axe falls now. - Dev

entries.title: Phase 4 Complete — Weapon Keywords & resolve_roll(): The Keyword Engine Achieves Final Form
notes: WEAPON skill_keyword FIELD — SUBSTRING MATCHING IS DEAD:
Every weapon struct now has a skill_keyword field: 'rifle', 'pistol', 'blade', 'brawl', etc.
Maps directly to keyword_tree.weapon_skills for INSTANT skill resolution
Old system: get_weapon_skill_match() scanned weapon NAME for substrings like 'pistol' or 'rifle'
New system: weapon.skill_keyword = 'pistol' → keyword_tree.weapon_skills.pistol → Modern Ranged Weapons / Pistol

add_weapon() signature: (stat, name, skill_keyword, dmg_o, dmg_g, dmg_a, range, damage_type)
ALL 27 CAREER WEAPON ARRAYS UPDATED:

9mm charge pistol → 'pistol' | 11mm charge rifle → 'rifle' | Combat knife → 'blade'

Render rifle → 'rifle' | Unarmed → 'brawl'

Export: [name, skill_keyword, dmg_o, dmg_g, dmg_a, range, type_string]
Import: detects array length — 7 elements = v4 (has keyword), 6 = v3 (infer keyword)
_infer_weapon_keyword() uses legacy substring matcher ONE LAST TIME to migrate old saves
Both statblock_export() and save_hero_and_track() write v4 format
Sgt Voss: rifle→'rifle', Combat knife→'blade', Unarmed→'brawl'
find_best_skill_for_weapon() REWRITTEN:
Step 1: Read weapon.skill_keyword
Step 2: Look up keyword_tree.weapon_skills[keyword] → { broad, spec }
Step 3: Try trained specialty → trained broad → untrained (half ability)
Step 4: Untrained ability looked up from keyword_tree.broads (not hardcoded 'dex' fallback)
Legacy fallback: if no skill_keyword, falls back to get_weapon_skill_match() substring matching
resolve_roll() — THE UNIFIED KEYWORD RESOLVER:
One function accepts natural-language keywords and returns a roll request struct:
'Perception'                    → build_skill_request(hero, 'Awareness', 'Perception', pen)
'Awareness'                     → build_skill_request(hero, 'Awareness', '', pen)
'STR feat'                      → build_feat_request(hero, 'str', 'STR', pen) — FULL ability score
'Initiative'                    → build_initiative_request(hero)
'9mm charge pistol attack'      → finds weapon by name → build_attack_request(hero, weapon, pen)
'Rifle'                         → keyword_tree.weapon_skills → Modern Ranged Weapons / Rifle
'Strength' or 'str'             → feat check for STR
Resolution order: feat suffix → initiative → attack suffix → spec skill → broad skill → weapon keyword → ability name
keyword_roll() — ONE-LINER CONVENIENCE:
keyword_roll(hero, 'Perception', 0) = meta_roll(hero, resolve_roll(hero, 'Perception', 0))
That's it. One function call. Keyword in, roll result out.
The entire roll pipeline: resolve keyword → build request → apply FX modifiers → apply wounds → roll dice → log result
careers_from_import() UPDATED for v4:
Detects 7-element weapon arrays (v4 with keyword) vs 6-element (v3 without)
v3 arrays auto-infer keyword via _infer_weapon_keyword() during import
Old careers.json files work — delete to regenerate with keywords
